mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding output capacity and economic strength, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO is above its 12-month moving average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5% indicates labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >50% of instances when UNRATE > 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising payroll employment and falling unemployment defines a robust labor market that supports consumer demand and equity valuations, producing positive returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint signal is non-positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates compress valuation multiples and slow economic activity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month regardless of the rate level.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in >50% of instances during Fed tightening cycles when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Higher retail sales signal strong consumer demand and robust economic activity, which bolsters corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns have no positive correlation with RSAFS levels (Spearman rho < 0.1 over any 1-year period).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,000 thousand units indicate a strong housing market that stimulates construction spending and consumer wealth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) during months when HOUST > 1000.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration of both GDP and industrial production confirms broad-based economic expansion, raising earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal has negative rank IC over any 3-year rolling walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5%, the labor market is tight; further declines risk wage-push inflation and Fed tightening, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in more than half of the periods when UNRATE < 5%.
